Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We need to interpret the event log and produce a POWL model that captures the observed behavior The log includes multiple cases with variations sometimes the process goes through IQC and maybe repeats IQC and AS multiple times sometimes it goes through RT and TST loops sometimes labeling LB is skipped sometimes no IQC RT at all

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We need to infer the control flow it appears that after OR MS QS are sequential The timestamps show OR then MS then QS always in that order and no concurrency observed They seem to happen in order and timestamps are increasing Typically OR then MS then QS So they are ordered

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Then we have a phase of Assembly AS and In line Quality Check IQC that may repeat In some cases we see patterns like AS then IQC then AS again maybe multiple times

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: It seems that IQC is a check after assembly if something fails it might require redoing assembly Actually the pattern After QS they do AS then IQC Then if IQC fails they go back to AS

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 5 after QS AS 1000 IQC 1015 AS 1030 IQC 1045 AS 1100 TST 1130 So pattern A B A B A That fits A B A B A

Name: Validation - NE
Text: But case 3 shows A B B A B A That deviates

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: That could be explained if the loop body is something like do AS then IQC one or more times then decide to repeat

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The process might be Order Received to Material Sourcing to Quality Screening to Assembly and In line Quality Check loop to Testing to Re Testing loop to Packaging to Labeling optional to Distribution Planning to Dispatch

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Observed sequences TST or TST RT TST or TST RT TST RT TST That is exactly a loop where after TST if fails you do RT and then loop back to TST

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Labeling LB is optional In case 2 LB is skipped So we need an XOR between LB and a silent transition skip

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: We could model the Assembly IQC rework loop as an outer loop with AS as first child and an inner loop over IQC as second child The inner loop would allow one or more IQC executions

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Inner loop equals loop of IQC with silent redo Outer loop equals loop of AS with inner loop as redo part This yields AS then one or more IQC then AS again and matches the observed variants including consecutive IQC

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 3 fits if after first AS the inner loop executes two IQC then returns to AS Then later it executes one IQC then returns to AS

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The high level flow is OR then MS then QS then assembly loop then testing loop then PK then labeling xor then DP then D

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: The logs do not show concurrency so a sequential StrictPartialOrder chain is sufficient

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: The final structure is transitions OR MS QS AS IQC TST RT PK LB DP D plus silent transitions for the inner IQC repetition and for skipping labeling Then define inner loop testing loop labeling xor and connect them in a root StrictPartialOrder

Name: Validation - PE
Text: This model covers case 4 with AS then direct exit to TST cases 1 and 2 with one IQC based rework case 5 with multiple testing reworks and optional labeling skip
